content:
5 AI Tools for Personalizing Customer Experiences

AI tools are reshaping how businesses connect with customers. Personalization isn't optional anymore - 71% of consumers expect it, and 76% get frustrated without it. Companies that leverage personalization see measurable gains, like a 40% revenue boost for fast-growing businesses. Tools like Netflix's recommendations save billions, and Amazon's AI assistant adds millions in profits.

Here are five standout AI platforms that help businesses deliver tailored experiences across channels:

  • Adobe Target: Advanced testing and real-time personalization for enterprises. Ideal for industries like retail and travel.
  • Dynamic Yield: Deep learning-powered recommendations and omnichannel personalization. Great for multi-channel operations.
  • CleverTap: Affordable and AI-driven, focusing on real-time data and predictive insights. Accessible for growing businesses.
  • Braze: AI tools for optimizing customer journeys across multiple channels. Perfect for mid-market and enterprise businesses.
  • Insider: AI-powered segmentation and journey orchestration with omnichannel support. Tailored for large organizations.

Quick Comparison:

Tool Key Features Pricing Model Free Trial Best For
Adobe Target Real-time testing, predictive insights Custom None Large enterprises
Dynamic Yield Deep learning, omnichannel personalization Custom 14 Days Multi-channel ops
CleverTap Predictive AI, real-time data $75+/month 30 Days Growing businesses
Braze Journey optimization, multi-channel AI Custom 14 Days Mid-market/enterprise
Insider AI segmentation, omnichannel journeys Custom None Large organizations

These tools can help businesses of all sizes improve customer engagement and drive revenue. Start with a free trial or demo to find the right fit for your needs.

1. Adobe Target

AI-Driven Personalization Features

Adobe Target leverages machine learning to assess visitor profiles and past behavior, tailoring content for each visitor in real time. Its Auto-Target feature ensures the delivery of the most relevant page version automatically, while Automated Personalization prioritizes offers and content that prompt key actions, such as adding items to a cart.

The platform’s Recommendations engine suggests products or content based on browsing and purchase history. Additionally, its Multi-Armed Bandit Testing (referred to as "Auto-allocate") dynamically redirects traffic toward experiences that perform best during live experiments. Marketers can also exclude certain data points, like device type or weather, to ensure the AI focuses on more impactful signals, such as customer loyalty or likelihood to convert.

Omnichannel Optimization Capabilities

Adobe Target works seamlessly with Adobe Experience Manager to deliver tailored content across websites, mobile apps, single-page applications, and IoT devices. This integration enables smooth transitions between channels - for example, connecting personalized mobile app offers with desktop homepage experiences. For travel and hospitality brands, this feature bridges the gap between call center interactions and online experiences. A representative from a major travel and hospitality company emphasized:

"A high priority for providing the best possible service for our guests is delivering the most relevant, personalized, frictionless experience at each moment within their journey with us, across all digital touch points" [3].

Real-Time Data Utilization

Adobe Target, in partnership with Adobe Real-Time CDP, enables "next-hit" personalization by reacting to customer actions during their session. For instance, if a customer contacts support, the system can immediately trigger a retention offer during their next visit - within the same session. The platform also distributes predictive audience segments to the edge network for evaluation in less than a second. An IDC report highlighted Adobe Target’s impressive results, including a 651% ROI over three years, a 38% rise in conversions, and a 125% increase in engagement [2]. These features make Adobe Target a go-to solution for businesses with complex personalization needs.

Ideal Business Size

Adobe Target is built for large-scale enterprises that demand advanced personalization across multiple digital channels. Its extensive features make it particularly suitable for industries like retail, financial services, and travel, where high traffic and sophisticated personalization strategies are essential.

2. Dynamic Yield

AI-Driven Personalization Features

Dynamic Yield, powered by its AdaptML™ deep learning engine, uses advanced technologies like NLP, RNNs, and Vision Transformers to automate decision-making processes [4][7]. Key features include:

  • NextML: Predicts the next best product to re-engage users effectively.
  • AffinityML: Leverages RNNs to analyze user behavior, distinguishing between one-time and complementary purchase patterns [4][7].
  • VisualML: Uses image recognition to recommend visually similar products, a game-changer for items without detailed metadata [4][7].
  • Shopping Muse: Provides a conversational shopping experience by understanding natural language queries and suggesting product bundles [4][8].
  • Predictive Targeting: Automatically identifies the best audience for specific campaigns [4].

These tools deliver measurable results. For instance, e.l.f. Cosmetics saw a 4.2% increase in ARPU and a 17.6% boost in conversion rates [5]. Similarly, Sky achieved a 140% increase in subscriber re-engagement through personalized offers [5].

Dynamic Yield’s strength lies not just in its features but also in its ability to provide seamless personalization across multiple channels.

Omnichannel Optimization Capabilities

With its Experience OS architecture, Dynamic Yield ensures personalized experiences across web platforms, mobile apps, email, SMS, push notifications, and even physical kiosks via APIs [4]. Electrolux, under the guidance of eCommerce Personalization and Growth Manager Marienza Benedetti, successfully implemented personalization across 21 markets and 16 languages, managing 63 domains without additional resources. This effort resulted in a 16% increase in direct-to-consumer revenue [9]. Similarly, LUISAVIAROMA integrated personalized recommendations into its mobile app in 2016, leading to a 25% rise in direct revenue and a 15% growth in ARPU from add-to-cart recommendations [5].

Ideal Business Size

Dynamic Yield is particularly well-suited for enterprises with complex, multi-channel operations. A great example is Tottenham Hotspur, which revamped its mobile website and optimized its conversion funnel using the platform, achieving full ROI within just 30 days [5]. The platform’s consistent recognition as a Leader in the Gartner® Magic Quadrant™ for Personalization Engines for seven consecutive years further highlights its reliability and effectiveness [5][6].

3. CleverTap

AI-Driven Personalization Features

CleverTap's CleverAI™ Decisioning Engine takes personalization to another level by using AI designed to actively achieve specific business goals [26, 30]. This engine operates through four specialized agents: Strategy Agents for planning and solving business challenges, Decision Agents to align products and offers with customer intent, Creative Agents for crafting tailored content, and Action Agents to facilitate one-on-one interactions [26, 30].

One standout feature is Scribe, which uses generative AI to create personalized messages and visuals for individual customers [29, 30]. Another is Intellinode, an AI-powered co-pilot that runs simultaneous experiments to uncover the best engagement strategies. This capability was spotlighted in the Forrester Wave™: Cross-Channel Marketing Hubs, Q4 2024 [12].

The results speak for themselves: businesses using these AI tools have reported a 66% jump in conversion rates, a 35% increase in marketing efficiency, and up to a 7X growth in customer lifetime value [12]. These features make CleverTap a powerful tool for delivering hyper-personalized customer experiences.

Real-Time Data Utilization

CleverTap goes beyond AI-driven personalization by tapping into real-time data to drive immediate, context-aware actions. Powered by its proprietary TesseractDB, the platform can analyze up to 10 years of customer data to predict outcomes and offer actionable insights [12]. This allows businesses to tailor messages based on live user behavior, while its "Best Time" algorithms ensure that nudges are delivered when customers are most likely to engage [27, 28].

For example, a leading streaming platform saw a 60% boost in campaign click-through rates by using these real-time capabilities [11].

"CleverTap helps us convert data into insights for more effective customer engagement." – Pallav Singhvi, Head of Growth [10]

By leveraging real-time personalization, businesses have achieved up to a 4X increase in click-through rates and accelerated purchase decisions by as much as 10X [10].

Ideal Business Size

CleverTap serves a diverse range of over 2,000 brands worldwide [27, 28]. With an Essentials plan starting at $75/month for up to 5,000 monthly active users, the platform is accessible to growing companies [14]. However, it truly shines as a solution for midsize and enterprise-level businesses that deal with large-scale data across industries like SaaS, healthcare, finance, media, and eCommerce [31, 32].

AI personalization strategies that work

sbb-itb-8feac72

4. Braze

Braze continues to push the boundaries of AI-powered personalization, offering a dynamic set of tools designed to elevate customer engagement on a large scale.

AI-Driven Personalization Features

The BrazeAI™ Suite takes personalization to the next level by automating tailored interactions using predictive, generative, and agentic intelligence. For example, its AI Item Recommendations tool analyzes purchase histories to suggest products likely to convert. Luxury retailer 24S (LVMH) implemented this feature and experienced a 35% increase in three-day purchase conversion rates over six months [19].

Another standout is the BrazeAI Decisioning Studio™, which acts as a continuous optimization engine. Using reinforcement learning, it autonomously determines the best channel, timing, frequency, and incentives for every user. This helped Kayo Sports expand from 300 to 1.2 million communication variations in fiscal year 2024, resulting in a 14% rise in subscriptions and a 105% jump in cross-sells [16].

"We are seeing 30% of additional conversion value. BrazeAI Decisioning Studio™ has proven to provide millions of dollars in customer lifetime value." – Mauricio Romero, Sr. Director AI & Advanced Analytics, Liberty Latin America [17]

Braze also offers Personalized Paths, which ensure every customer receives the most relevant message, creative, channel, or offer at each stage of their journey.

Omnichannel Optimization Capabilities

Braze integrates seamlessly across multiple channels, including email, mobile push, in-app messages, SMS, web, WhatsApp, and Content Cards. All of this is managed through a single unified customer profile. With Braze Canvas, businesses can create real-time, adaptive cross-channel campaigns. Research shows that adding a second messaging channel can amplify purchases by up to 4.5× [15][21].

One example of Braze's impact is KFC Spain's "Fries Compensation" campaign. By delivering personalized apologies and free orders through email, push notifications, and in-app messages, the campaign achieved a 95% open rate, a 679% increase in app downloads, and a 233% boost in daily active users [22].

Braze’s Intelligent Channel feature further enhances engagement by identifying each user's preferred platform, ensuring messages are delivered where they’re most likely to resonate. This is particularly effective for strategies that merge physical and digital experiences (phygital touchpoints) [19].

Real-Time Data Utilization

Braze excels at processing real-time behavioral data, instantly updating unified customer profiles. Its Connected Content feature allows businesses to pull live data - like stock levels, local weather, or sports scores - directly from external APIs when sending messages. Pizza Hut used this feature with multi-armed bandit testing to evaluate hundreds of email variations, leading to a 30% increase in transactions, a 21% revenue lift, and a 10% boost in profit [18].

The Intelligent Timing feature further refines engagement by analyzing customer behavior to pinpoint the best time to send messages [20][22]. For instance, New Zealand-based OneRoof combined Intelligent Timing with Connected Content to deliver personalized property listings, achieving a 218% increase in total clicks and a 23% rise in email click-to-open rates [18]. Like Adobe Target and Dynamic Yield, Braze demonstrates the power of real-time data in delivering adaptive, cross-channel personalization.

Ideal Business Size

Braze caters to global mid-market and enterprise businesses that require advanced personalization capabilities. It’s especially effective for industries such as retail, eCommerce, media, streaming, quick-service restaurants, travel, beauty, and SaaS [15][18][19][23].

For example, Canva used Braze to scale its weekly email volume from 30 million to 50 million while maintaining a 99% deliverability rate. By leveraging Connected Content for real-time localization in over 20 languages, Canva achieved a 33% increase in open rates [16].

5. Insider

Rounding out the list, Insider provides an AI-powered platform designed to create personalized customer experiences across multiple channels. It integrates Agentic, Generative, and Predictive AI to streamline customer segmentation and journey orchestration [27][13].

AI-Driven Personalization Features

Insider's standout features revolve around its ability to tailor customer experiences with precision.

The Sirius AI™ Suite, for instance, enhances marketer efficiency by 60%, using predictive segmentation to identify key audience groups based on factors like purchase likelihood, churn risk, discount preferences, and potential lifetime value [24][26][28]. Its generative AI takes personalization a step further by crafting campaign copy, visuals, and entire customer journeys from simple prompts, identifying lucrative segments up to 30 times faster than traditional methods [24][13].

The platform also offers over 20 Smart Recommendation strategies that automatically adjust based on user preferences and product attributes [24][25][29]. Tools like Eureka AI Search provide highly relevant search results by analyzing past actions and predicting future behaviors, helping to lower abandonment rates [24][13][29]. Additionally, InStory brings Instagram-like story features to mobile sites and apps, boosting product discovery. For example, Dover Saddlery used InStory to deliver personalized, bite-sized stories, generating $1.7 million in extra revenue [13][29].

"By combining reliable customer data with powerful technology that stitches beautiful customer journeys together, Insider has helped our business connect with our customers in a deeper, more meaningful way." – Digital Marketing Manager, Leading European Retailer [24]

Omnichannel Optimization Capabilities

Insider supports a wide range of communication channels - over 12 in total - including web, mobile apps, email, SMS, WhatsApp, RCS, and push notifications [26][27]. Its Send-Time Optimization feature ensures messages reach customers at the most engaging moments [26][13][29].

For instance, Slazenger leveraged Insider One to run targeted campaigns across 30 customer segments, achieving a 700% increase in customer acquisition and a 49X return on investment (ROI) in just two months [26][13][28]. Similarly, Carrefour used personalized web push notifications to target cart abandoners, resulting in a 350% boost in conversion rates and generating $11,000 in additional revenue within the first month [28].

"Insider One enabled us to build relevant and timely campaigns across nearly 30 different customer segments. We built personalized, omnichannel user experiences... which helped us gain a 700% increase in customer acquisition and 49X ROI within just two months." – Ecommerce Director, Slazenger [26]

Real-Time Data Utilization

Insider's Actionable Customer Data Platform brings together data from both online and offline sources - such as CRM systems, point-of-sale (POS) data, and contact centers - to provide a comprehensive view of both known and anonymous users [24][27][28]. Even visitors without shared contact details can receive tailored experiences thanks to real-time profile creation [24][29].

The results speak for themselves. Philips implemented Insider's AI-driven personalization and Smart Recommender on its website, achieving a 40.11% increase in conversion rates and a 35% rise in average order value [13][29]. Meanwhile, Lenovo used personalized web push notifications to re-engage customers who had abandoned their carts, reducing abandonment rates by over 10% [13][29].

Ideal Business Size

Insider is tailored for midsize and enterprise-level businesses that require advanced omnichannel engagement tools [13][29]. With a stellar 4.9/5 rating on Gartner Peer Insights from over 2,000 users and recognition as the #1 Leader in 11 categories in G2's Winter '26 reports, the platform has proven its value for organizations with complex customer interaction needs [25][27].

Feature Comparison

Choosing the right AI personalization tool depends on factors like your budget, team size, and technical know-how. Here's a quick breakdown of five popular platforms to help you decide.

CleverTap is the most budget-friendly option, starting at $75/month and offering a generous 30-day free trial [1]. This makes it a great choice for mid-market businesses looking to explore AI personalization without significant upfront costs. On the other hand, Adobe Target, Dynamic Yield, Braze, and Insider follow custom pricing models tailored to enterprise-level needs [1]. While this approach works well for large organizations with complex requirements, it can make cost planning tricky for smaller teams.

Trial periods also vary across platforms. CleverTap offers a 30-day trial, while Dynamic Yield and Braze provide 14-day trials. However, Adobe Target and Insider require a sales demo instead of a trial [1]. Short trial periods can pose challenges for teams managing more complex integrations.

Tool Key AI Features Pricing Model Free Trial Recommended Business Size
Adobe Target Adobe Sensei AI, Auto-Target, Automated Personalization Custom None Enterprise
Dynamic Yield Experience OS, Shopping Muse AI, AdaptML Custom 14 Days Large / Enterprise
CleverTap Clever.AI, Predictive Segmentation, Scribe GenAI Starts at $75/mo 30 Days Mid-market / Enterprise
Braze Sage AI, Predictive Targeting, Journey Orchestration Custom 14 Days Mid-market / Enterprise
Insider Sirius AI, Generative Journeys, WhatsApp Commerce Custom None Mid-market / Enterprise

Implementation is another critical factor to consider. Adobe Target and Dynamic Yield often require developer involvement for setup, while Insider and Braze offer marketer-friendly drag-and-drop tools [1][30][13]. If your team lacks technical expertise, these user-friendly platforms might be a better fit. Additionally, for omnichannel campaigns that include WhatsApp, SMS, and mobile apps, Insider and Braze deliver strong capabilities [1][13].

Conclusion

AI-powered personalization tools are proving to be game-changers for businesses ready to embrace them. Companies leveraging hyper-personalization are seeing tangible results - fast-growing organizations, for instance, report generating 40% more revenue compared to their competitors. Similarly, brands that incorporate a second messaging channel experience a 4.5X increase in purchases per user[1]. These tools aren't just about improving customer engagement - they're about driving real revenue growth.

Choosing the right tool, however, depends on aligning a platform's capabilities with your business's specific needs and technical resources. For mid-sized businesses exploring personalization, CleverTap offers an accessible entry point with plans starting at $75/month and a 30-day trial[1]. On the other hand, enterprise-level teams dealing with more complex demands might gravitate toward Adobe Target's advanced testing features or Insider's comprehensive omnichannel approach.

"The ease of setting up experiments without needing deep technical expertise is also a big plus." – Art B., Conversion Rate Optimization Manager[1]

Free trials can be a great way to determine whether a platform meets your needs. Different trial periods give you the flexibility to test and identify the best fit for your business. To see results quickly, focus on impactful use cases like abandoned cart recovery or predictive offer recommendations - these can deliver noticeable improvements in conversion rates almost immediately[15].

The demand for better, more personalized customer experiences is only growing. With the right AI tools, you can meet those expectations while scaling your efforts effectively. Take the time to evaluate these options and align your personalization strategy with outcomes that truly matter for your business.

FAQs

How can AI tools help businesses create personalized customer experiences?

AI tools are changing the way businesses connect with their customers by turning data - like browsing habits, purchase trends, and past interactions - into personalized, real-time experiences. Through machine learning, these tools can spot patterns and predict what a customer is likely to value most. This allows businesses to serve up tailored content, product recommendations, and messages instantly.

When customers feel that a brand "gets" them, they’re more likely to engage. The result? Higher click-through rates, longer time spent on sites, and repeat purchases. In fact, research shows that 81% of U.S. consumers prefer brands that provide personalized experiences. What’s more, AI keeps learning from every interaction, helping businesses stay in tune with customer needs and deliver even better experiences over time.

For those ready to harness these tools, Tech Leaders offers training in engineering leadership and AI business strategy. This equips professionals with the know-how to deploy and scale AI solutions effectively, ensuring they make the biggest impact possible.

What should businesses look for when selecting an AI tool for customer personalization?

When selecting an AI personalization tool, there are a few essential factors businesses should keep in mind to ensure it aligns with their goals. First, data integration is a must. The tool should effortlessly pull together data from various sources - like browsing history, purchase records, and CRM systems - to deliver real-time, tailored recommendations. Equally important are the tool's AI capabilities. Advanced machine learning features can adapt to customer behavior, offering personalized experiences across different channels.

Other key aspects to consider include scalability, which ensures the tool can handle increasing data and traffic as your business grows, and privacy and compliance, which guarantees responsible data use in line with regulations like GDPR and CCPA. Additionally, ease of implementation plays a big role - tools with intuitive interfaces and pre-built templates can save time and reduce complexity. And, of course, the cost structure should fit your budget while delivering measurable ROI, supported by features like built-in analytics to track performance.

For those aiming to align technical decisions with broader business objectives, Tech Leaders offers training programs designed to bridge the gap between technical know-how and strategic planning. These programs equip teams to effectively implement and optimize AI personalization tools.

Why is real-time data crucial for AI-powered personalization?

Real-time data gives AI the ability to adjust on the fly based on a customer’s actions at that very moment. This means personalized recommendations, content, or offers stay relevant and hit the mark when it matters most. The result? Better engagement, higher conversion rates, and happier customers.

When businesses tap into real-time insights, they can craft more impactful interactions, cut down on avoidable expenses, and build lasting customer loyalty. It’s about meeting people where they are - right when they need it.
description: Compare five AI platforms that enable real-time, omnichannel personalization, recommendations, and journey optimization to boost engagement and revenue.

title: 5 AI Tools for Personalizing Customer Experiences
